title: Certificate digital seal (HMAC) + tamper-evident public verification
business_reason: Public verification (FR-0004) looked up a code and returned whitelisted fields but offered no integrity proof — a stored public_verification row could be altered without detection. Add an HMAC seal over the public fields, set at publish, that /verify recomputes: integrity_verified is true only if nothing was tampered, and the seal cannot be forged without the server secret. Strengthens the certificate trust anchor (digital_signature_policy).

design.helpers: server/eval/certificate.ts: PURE certificateSeal(fields, secret) = HMAC-SHA256 over a canonical string of the whitelisted public fields (verification_code, candidate_name, olympiad_name, award, issued_on, status); verifyCertificateSeal(fields, seal). Unit-tested (deterministic, changes with any field, changes with secret, true/false).
design.storage: migration 0024: public_verification.content_hash. certEffects publish computes + stores the seal; revoke RE-SEALS with the new status (so a legitimately revoked cert still passes integrity).
design.verify: publicVerificationResponse adds integrity_verified (recompute vs stored content_hash); the verify route + public verify page show a '🔒 Integrity verified' / '⚠ may have been altered' badge.
design.secret: CERT_SIGNING_SECRET env (dev fallback); must be set in prod.

param_14.question: How is a published certificate's public record protected from tampering?
param_14.answer: At publish, the server computes an HMAC seal over the certificate's whitelisted public fields and stores it. The public /verify endpoint recomputes the seal and returns integrity_verified — true only when the stored fields are unaltered. An attacker cannot forge a valid seal without the server signing secret, so editing a public_verification field is detectable.
param_14.security_implications: tamper-evidence on the public record (HMAC, unforgeable without CERT_SIGNING_SECRET); revoke re-seals so legitimate state changes pass; integrity_verified is a non-PII boolean safe to expose; the seal covers only whitelisted public fields (no private data); secret must be set in prod (dev fallback documented).
param_14.downstream_dependencies: complements the QR + public verify page (FR-0004/0005); a future move to asymmetric signatures (public-key verifiable offline) can replace the HMAC.
param_14.assumptions: the seal covers the same fields /verify returns; CERT_SIGNING_SECRET is stable across the publish + verify lifetime.
needs_user_review: adopt asymmetric (public-key) certificate signatures for offline/third-party verification?
